title: grindelia nana var. integerrima
intro: Grindelia nana var. integerrima, also known as Gumweed, is a plant species native to the western United States and Mexico, belonging to the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant is rich in sesquiterpene lactones and flavonoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Grindelia nana var. integerrima include soothing respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma, as well as alleviating skin irritations and wounds. Herbal preparations of the plant, often in the form of infusions or tinctures, are traditionally used to support immune function and promote overall well-being.
